behaviors_covered: MemorySnapshot field-wise subtraction; auto_measure stays False on diff results
memory_profiling worked example from vLLM mem_utils.py:L209-L235 reproduces 6 GiB non_kv_cache
page_size formula (2 * block_size * num_kv_heads * head_size * dtype_bytes); demo's 64 KiB pinned
get_num_blocks: integer divide by page_size and num_layers, clamps negatives to 0, wasted < page_size*num_layers
FullAttentionSpec.head_size_v defaults to head_size; max_memory_usage_bytes rounds up
FreeKVCacheBlockQueue: O(1) popleft/append/remove (head/middle/tail), empty raises, double-remove raises
KVCacheBlock: hash setter asserts existing-None; reset_hash clears
BlockPool: null block reservation (id=0, is_null=True, popped at __init__), get_usage subtracts null
BlockPool.get_new_blocks: ref_cnt bump, oversubscribe raises, evicts cached LRU blocks
BlockPool.free_blocks: ref_cnt drop, only ref_cnt=0 blocks return to queue, cached hash persists
BlockPool.touch: re-activates freed-but-cached block in O(1), skips null block, skips already-allocated blocks
BlockPool.evict_blocks: drops cache hash without freeing (KV connector path)
BlockPool.reset_prefix_cache: only succeeds when 1 block in use (the null)
enable_caching=False: cache_block / get_cached_block become no-ops
determine_available_memory: 35,148 num_gpu_blocks for Llama-3.2-1B on H100 reproduces exactly
Block-size sweep: {8: 70297, 16: 35148, 32: 17574, 64: 8787} matches demo table
Util margin = 6.4 GiB on 80 GiB H100 (8% safety budget)
estimate_max_concurrency: 275 concurrent at avg_seq_len=2048 (demo number)
Negative-KV clamp to 0 (OOM guard)
PreemptionScenario.kv_bytes = 1 GiB exact for 8K/32/8/128/fp16
recompute_seconds=163.84 ms vs swap_seconds=62.5 ms; recompute_is_faster=False (K06)
Cross-chapter: Ch04 SimpleKVCacheManager import doesn't collide with Ch05's implementation package
End-to-end demo workflow: profile → layout → BlockPool → cache → free → re-allocate

wisdom_applied: W02 (preemption-test design): not directly applicable to Ch05 (no preemption test); however the broader principle 'don't let the test pass for the wrong reason' is honored — every demo number is asserted exactly, not just 'something positive'.
knowledge_applied: K04 (null block at id 0): test_null_block_reserved_at_id_zero, test_first_allocated_block_is_id_one, test_get_usage_subtracts_null_block
K05 (doubly-linked queue O(1) middle remove): full TestFreeQueueRemoveMiddle suite
K06 (recompute-only preemption in v1): test_recompute_is_slower_for_8k confirms swap is faster on raw latency, vLLM still chose recompute
K08 (cache_block keeps block in cache after free): test_cache_hash_persists_after_free + test_get_new_blocks_evicts_cached_lru

K09 (cudagraph subtraction is opt-in): not directly tested (env-var path); cudagraph_memory IS subtracted in our determine_available_memory unconditionally — flagged in K17 (see fidelity findings, none blocking)
K10 (allocate_slots arg shape): not applicable (Ch05 uses BlockPool.get_new_blocks, full KVCacheManager is Ch12-13)
